---
title: "Southeastern Sneezeweed (Helenium pinnatifidum)"
slug: "helenium-pinnatifidum"
group: "Plantae"
image: "https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/621877605/medium.jpg"        # keep for thumbnails
image-alt: "Southeastern Sneezeweed"  # keep for a11y
tax_kingdom: "Plantae"
tax_class: "Magnoliopsida"
tax_order: "Asterales"
tax_family: "Asteraceae"
categories: ["Plantae", "Magnoliopsida", "Asterales", "Asteraceae"]   # these drive the chips + filtering
freeze: true
---

<figure class="species-figure">

<img src="https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/621877605/medium.jpg" alt="Southeastern Sneezeweed" class="species-photo"/>

<figcaption class="photo-credit">

Photo: <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/people/rangerval">
rangerval</a> (CC BY-NC) via <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/observations/341589428">iNaturalist</a>

</figcaption>

</figure>

## Taxonomy

-   **Class:** Magnoliopsida
-   **Order:** Asterales
-   **Family:** Asteraceae

## Occurrence at DeLuca Preserve

-   [DeLuca Bio Blitz Species Observations](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?project_id=191727&subview=map&taxon_id=163590)

## Conservation & rarity context

-   IUCN: Not Listed
-   **Rarity note:** Common species in Florida found in moist to wet habitats.

## Natural history notes

A perennial wildflower characterized by its deeply divided leaves and yellow flower heads with drooping ray petals surrounding a rounded central disk. In Florida, it is found in moist to wet habitats such as flatwoods, wet prairies, and roadside ditches. It typically blooms during the warmer months and provides a nectar source for a variety of pollinators.

## References / links

-   [iNaturalist species page](https://www.inaturalist.org/taxa/163590-Helenium-pinnatifidum)
-   [Local profile](https://www.flawildflowers.org/flower-friday-helenium-pinnatifidum/)
